metadata.description: Converts unstructured text to first-order logic using neuro-symbolic methods

examples.input: Alice is a parent of Bob. Bob is a parent of Charlie. Therefore, Alice is a grandparent of Charlie.
examples.output: parent(a, bob.). parent(a, charlie.). parent(grand, charlie.). grandparent(alice, charlie).
examples.predict_our_method: parent(a, bob.). parent(a, charlie.). parent(grand, charlie.). grandparent(alice, charlie).
examples.predict_baseline: grandparent(alice, charlie). parent(alice, bob). parent(bob, charlie).
examples.metadata_trace: ["Input text: Alice is a parent of Bob. Bob is a parent of Charlie. Therefore, Alice is a grandparent of Charlie....", "Extracted logic: parent(a, bob.). parent(a, charlie.). parent(grand, charlie.). grandparent(alice, charlie).", "Validated: True"]

examples.input: All humans are mortal. Socrates is a human. Therefore, Socrates is mortal.
examples.metadata_trace: ["Input text: All humans are mortal. Socrates is a human. Therefore, Socrates is mortal....", "Extracted logic: mortal(socrates).", "Validated: True"]

examples.input: If it rains, the ground gets wet. It is raining. Therefore, the ground is wet.
examples.metadata_trace: ["Input text: If it rains, the ground gets wet. It is raining. Therefore, the ground is wet....", "Extracted logic: wet(ground).", "Validated: True"]
